steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: What does red-shift in the light from a distant galaxy tell us?
options: The galaxy is moving towards us, compressing the light waves
The galaxy contains more red stars than blue stars
The galaxy is moving away from us, stretching the light to longer wavelengths
The galaxy is rotating very quickly, changing its colour
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Red-shift means the light has been stretched to longer wavelengths (towards the red end of the spectrum) because the galaxy is moving away from us. This is the Doppler effect applied to light — nothing to do with the colour of the stars.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Red-shift means the galaxy is moving away from us. The faster it moves away, the greater the red-shift observed.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.8.3 — Red-shift

steps.title: Red-shift
steps.bodyHTML: When a galaxy moves away from us, the light it emits is stretched to longer wavelengths, shifting it towards the red end of the spectrum — this is called red-shift. Astronomers observe that almost all distant galaxies show red-shift, and that more distant galaxies show greater red-shift, meaning they are moving away faster. This is key evidence that the universe is expanding.
commonErrors: Thinking red-shift means the galaxy is physically red in colour
Forgetting that blue-shift (light moving towards us) also exists — a few nearby galaxies show this
Confusing red-shift with evidence for the Big Bang itself (it is evidence for expansion, which supports the Big Bang)

aqa.questionText: Astronomers observe that light from a distant galaxy has a longer wavelength than the same light produced in a laboratory. Explain what this tells us about the galaxy and about the universe.

steps.label: Identify red-shift
steps.content: The light has been shifted to longer wavelengths — this is called red-shift.

steps.label: Explain what it tells us about the galaxy
steps.content: The galaxy is moving away from us (receding). The greater the red-shift, the faster the galaxy is moving away.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — galaxy is moving away from us

steps.label: Explain what it tells us about the universe
steps.content: Since almost all galaxies show red-shift, the universe is expanding in all directions.

aqa.finalAnswer: Red-shift detected; galaxy is moving away; universe is expanding.

aqa.questionText: A galaxy that is further away from Earth shows a greater red-shift than a nearer galaxy. What does this indicate?

options.label: A
options.text: The further galaxy is moving towards us more quickly

options.label: B
options.text: The further galaxy contains hotter, redder stars

options.label: C
options.text: The further galaxy is moving away from us more quickly

options.label: D
options.text: The further galaxy is the same distance away but emits lower-energy light

markSchemeSteps: Greater red-shift = greater recession speed — the further galaxy is moving away faster ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option A (blue-shift) would indicate motion towards us — red-shift always indicates recession.
